skill.name: handoff
skill.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.
skill.summary: Generate conversation summaries for AI agent handoffs.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: All four static analysis findings are false positives. The two HIGH-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts correspond to YAML frontmatter and a conditional logic line that contain no cryptographic operations. The MEDIUM-severity external_commands finding refers to mktemp usage for legitimate temporary file creation, which is the designed behavior of this handoff skill. The LOW-severity filesystem finding points to the same legitimate temp file creation. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was detected.

content.user_title: Hand Off Conversations Between AI Agents
content.value_statement: AI agents lose context between sessions. This skill creates a compact handoff document so a new agent can continue your work without starting over.
actual_capabilities: Generates a handoff document summarizing the current conversation for a new AI agent
Saves the handoff to a temporary file for easy retrieval in the next session
Suggests relevant skills for the next session based on work in progress
Tailors the handoff content based on user-specified focus for the next session
References existing artifacts by path or URL instead of duplicating content
Avoids unnecessary duplication of information already captured in other documents
limitations: Requires the next session to have access to the saved handoff file
Cannot transfer in-progress code or terminal state between sessions
May not capture implicit context that was not discussed in the conversation
Works best with structured project workflows that produce artifacts

use_cases.title: Continue coding across multiple sessions
use_cases.description: Pass context between AI coding sessions without losing progress on long-running development tasks.
use_cases.target_user: Software developer working on long-running coding tasks

use_cases.title: Hand off research and documentation work
use_cases.description: Transfer research findings, draft content, and editorial decisions between writing sessions.

use_cases.title: Transfer project planning context
use_cases.description: Move project decisions, meeting notes, and planning artifacts between coordination sessions.
use_cases.target_user: Project manager coordinating work across multiple sessions

prompt_templates.title: Basic handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a handoff document summarizing our work so a new agent can continue from here.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new session and need to preserve current context

prompt_templates.title: Focused handoff with next session goal
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff for the next session, which will focus on implementing the payment API.
prompt_templates.scenario: Next session has a specific objective

prompt_templates.title: Artifact-aware handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a handoff document. Reference existing artifacts by path or URL instead of duplicating content.
prompt_templates.scenario: Project already has documentation such as PRDs or ADRs

prompt_templates.title: Multi-skill handoff with tool suggestions
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff document and suggest which skills the next session should load to continue the work.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex project that uses multiple AI skills

output_examples.input: Summarize our work on the authentication system so far for the next session.
output: A markdown handoff document with sections for: current state of the authentication system, decisions made, open questions, and recommended skills for the next session.

output_examples.input: Create a handoff for a new developer who needs to implement the forgot-password feature.
output: A tailored handoff document including: the project context, existing authentication architecture, requirements for forgot-password, reproduction steps for the current bug, and relevant file paths to reference.

best_practices: Create artifacts such as PRDs and ADRs during your session so the handoff can reference them by URL instead of duplicating content.
Provide a specific focus description when the next session has a clear goal to produce a more targeted handoff document.
Review the generated handoff file before starting the next session to verify all needed context is included.
anti_patterns: Relying on the handoff to transfer in-progress code changes instead of using version control for code management.
Duplicating information from existing artifacts in the handoff document instead of referencing them by path or URL.
Creating a handoff without specifying what the next session should focus on, resulting in a vague document.

faq.question: What is a handoff document?
faq.answer: A handoff document summarizes the current conversation and project state so a new AI agent can continue the work without losing context.

faq.question: Where is the handoff file saved?
faq.answer: The file is saved to a temporary directory. After the handoff is created, ask the agent for the file path to locate it.

faq.question: Can I use a handoff with a different AI tool?
faq.answer: Yes. The handoff file is a plain markdown document that can be read by any AI tool in a new session.

faq.question: Does the handoff replace version control?
faq.answer: No. Use version control for code changes and the handoff only for conversation context and session continuity.

faq.question: What should I include in the focus description?
faq.answer: Describe what the next session will work on, such as implementing a specific feature or fixing a known bug.

faq.question: Can I create multiple handoffs in one session?
faq.answer: Yes. Each handoff creates a new file with a unique name generated by mktemp.
